AccountID.description: Account ID that created the VPC

IP.description: IP address of the external DNS server for the VPC

Port.description: Port of the external DNS server for the VPC

Key.description: Tag key for the user tag

Value.description: Tag value for the user tag

CreatedTime.description: Creation time of the forwarding rule

Enable.description: Whether the forwarding rule is enabled. true: enabled. false: disabled

EndpointID.description: Endpoint ID. This parameter is only valid and required when the Type parameter is OUTBOUND

ForwardIPs.description: IP address and port of the external DNS server. You can add up to 10 IP addresses. This parameter is only valid and required when the Type parameter is OUTBOUND

LastOperator.description: Account ID of the last update to the forwarding rule

Line.description: Carrier for the outbound IP address of the recursive DNS server. This parameter is only valid when the Type parameter is LINE. Supported values: Mobile: China Mobile, Telecom: China Telecom, Unicom: China Unicom

Name.description: Name of the forwarding rule. Supports UTF-8 format

ProjectName.description: Project name associated with the forwarding rule. Default is default

Tags.description: One or more tags associated with the forwarding rule

Type.description: Forwarding rule type. OUTBOUND: Forward to external DNS server. LINE: Carrier for the outbound IP address of the custom public recursive DNS server

UpdatedTime.description: Update time of the forwarding rule

VPCs.description: VPC associated with the forwarding rule. The forwarding rule takes effect in the associated VPC. When the Type parameter is OUTBOUND, the VPC region must match the region of the endpoint

ZoneName.description: Domain name(s) associated with the forwarding rule. You can enter one or more domain names. Separate multiple domain names with English commas. Up to 500 domain names are supported. This parameter is only valid and required when the Type parameter is OUTBOUND. If you set this parameter to *, the forwarding rule applies to all domain names associated with the VPC
